dump_session.notes: 3C M25A10PA T1 was first inserted according to inferred markings and read as all 0xff under multiple 27C256-compatible profiles. Reversing the chip orientation produced plausible data; two read-only 27C256@DIP28 reads matched byte-for-byte. 4C M10A10LA EDH was first read successfully as 27C256@DIP28 and as a stable 27C512@DIP28 A16-low bank. Native T48 AT27C011/D27C011 reads mirrored that 64 KB bank, but patched custom PROM bitbang profiles captured a distinct pin-22-high/A16-high bank and a complete 128 KB image. Chip ID reads reported 0x0000, expected for these custom mask ROMs or unsupported ID path.

dumps.notes: Program PROM dump. Disassembled with ../mame/unidasm -arch upd7810 -basepc 0x0000. First bytes decode as DI; JMP $0180, matching a plausible uPD7810 reset/vector table.

dumps.expected_size: 128 KB / 1 Mbit. Schematic evidence identifies 4C pin 1 as A15, J5 ties A15 to BK2 on the 6C E01A05KA, J6 wires pin 20 /CE, and the hard-to-read A16/OE label appears to be A16 wired to BK3. A patched minipro custom PROM profile that treats pin 22 as A16 produced a 128 KB image whose low half matches the stable 27C512 capture and whose high half matches a repeated pin-22-high custom read.
dumps.programmer_device_type: LQ500_4C_A16_PIN22@DIP28 custom PROM profile on patched minipro/T48

test_reads.interpretation: Original 27C256-compatible read. It matches the A15-high 32 KB quarter of the 128 KB custom PROM capture.

split_banks.notes: Stable across repeated 27C512 reads. Contains plausible bitmap/font data and Draft strings. Schematic evidence reportedly identifies 4C pin 1 as A15, so this is the low 32 KB address half of the 64 KB read.
Matches the canonical 27C256@DIP28 dump exactly. Schematic evidence reportedly identifies 4C pin 1 as A15, so this is the high 32 KB address half of the 64 KB read.

test_reads.interpretation: Upper 32768 bytes match the original 27C256@DIP28 dump exactly; lower 32768 bytes are different stable output when the 27C512 profile drives pin 1/A15 low. A board continuity check found 4C pin 1 is not tied to VCC/pin 28, unlike 3C, and the schematic reportedly confirms pin 1 is A15. The schematic/jumper trace shows J5 tying pin 1/A15 to BK2 on the 6C E01A05KA, J6 wiring pin 20 /CE, and the hard-to-read A16/OE label appears to be A16 wired to BK3. This 65536-byte read is the stable 4C A16-low image.

test_reads.programmer_device_type: LQ500_4C_PIN22_HIGH@DIP28 custom PROM profile on patched minipro/T48
test_reads.interpretation: Custom bitbang PROM read with pin 20 low and pin 22 held high. Two reads matched byte-for-byte. This 65536-byte read is the stable 4C A16-high image.

test_reads.programmer_device_type: AT27C011@DIP28 on T48; D27C011@DIP28 also tested and matched
test_reads.interpretation: T48 accepted both AT27C011@DIP28 and D27C011@DIP28 reads despite minipro -d reporting those profiles as T56-only. Both 128 KB reads matched each other exactly. Splitting the result into 64 KB halves gives two byte-for-byte copies of the 27C512 capture; splitting into 32 KB quarters gives A15-low, A15-high, A15-low, A15-high. This does not expose distinct BK3/A16 data.

dumps.notes: Resident CG/font ROM. Byte patterns look like glyph data. The A16-low/A15-high/original-27C256 quarter includes Draft and Roman strings; the A16-low/A15-low quarter includes Draft strings; the A16-high bank includes Sans Serif strings at full-image offsets 164CBh, 18E13h, 1A570h, and 1C5D3h. Chip ID mismatch/0x0000 was bypassed with -y. Native T48 27C011-style reads did not toggle the relevant pin for this custom mask ROM, but patched custom PROM bitbang profiles captured a distinct pin-22-high bank and a 128 KB image ordered as A16-low followed by A16-high.

dumps.expected_size: confirm from chip markings; 4M label likely means 4 Mbit if populated
dumps.notes: Pending if populated. Character generator select; banked into 8000h-A000h. Board photos suggest this footprint may be unpopulated and labeled 4M/2M/1M/256kbit MASK IMPROM.
Use for optional font module or any extra CG ROM present.
